explanations.Betting Spread: Vegas point spread — the single strongest predictor. Incorporates injury news, matchup context, and public/sharp money that pure stats miss.
explanations.Coach PAKE: Coaching quality measured by historical post-season performance. Tournament experience matters.
explanations.Strength of Schedule: How tough a team's regular season opponents were. Battle-tested teams fare better.
explanations.Barthag (Power Rating): Barttorvik's power rating — estimated probability of beating an average D1 team.
explanations.Effective FG%: Shooting efficiency adjusted for 3-pointers being worth more. Better shooters advance.
explanations.Offensive Rating: Points scored per 100 possessions. Efficient offenses win tournament games.
explanations.KenPom Efficiency Margin: The gap between a team's offensive and defensive efficiency — the gold standard power rating.
explanations.Defensive Rating: Points allowed per 100 possessions. Tournament games are lower-scoring — defense travels.
explanations.Tempo: Pace of play. Faster-tempo teams tend to underperform in March — the model penalizes this.
explanations.Tournament Round: Later rounds favor stronger teams — upsets are more common early.
explanations.3-Point Defense %: How well a team defends the three-point line. Limiting threes matters in single-elimination.
explanations.Seed Difference: Higher seeds (lower numbers) win more often, but carries less weight than you'd think.
explanations.Road Win %: Winning away from home. Tournament games are on neutral courts — road warriors adapt.
explanations.Defensive Turnover %: How often a team forces turnovers. Disruptive defenses create extra possessions.
explanations.Close Game Win %: Win percentage in games decided by 5 or fewer points. Clutch play under pressure.
explanations.Win Percentage: Overall regular season win rate. A baseline signal for team quality.
